posture: External references sharpen NHS App process terminology and browser-evidence technique only; local phase blueprints remain authoritative.

borrowed: eligibility depends on patient-facing personalised services, NHS login posture, procurement/framework alignment, standards compliance, and NHS App team prioritisation
delivery proceeds through product assessment, Solution Design, Sandpit, AOS, incident rehearsal, SCAL, connection agreement, limited release, full release, and post-live obligations
applied: Phase 7 is opened only for contract and implementation work; Sandpit, AOS, SCAL, connection agreement, limited release, and go-live remain deferred or blocked until their owner tracks produce evidence
rejected: treating the Phase 6 exit verdict as NHS App go-live approval

borrowed: NHS App integrations must meet WCAG 2.2 AA, the NHS service standard, clinical safety standards, and data privacy standards through SCAL evidence
applied: manual accessibility, SCAL, DCB0129, DCB0160, GDPR, and PECR remain Phase 7 release constraints rather than immediate implementation blockers
rejected: allowing local Playwright proof to replace an NHS App accessibility audit or clinical-safety sign-off

borrowed: expression-of-interest readiness asks for service-desk posture, demo environment access, recent user research, recent accessibility audit, and committed design and development capacity
applied: track 374 must freeze onboarding-pack and service-desk evidence inputs before downstream implementation treats the manifest as externally ready
rejected: burying public service desk, demo environment, or user-research needs as non-engineering notes

borrowed: NHS App web integration runs supplier pages in a tailored webview, with hidden supplier headers, NHS App chrome, custom user-agent handling, JS API functionality, and mapped jump-off points
applied: tracks 374, 375, 377, and 378 are opened because manifest truth, jump-off mapping, channel context, and shell policy are the first load-bearing seams
rejected: using user-agent presence alone as trusted embedded-context proof

borrowed: NHS App can pass assertedLoginIdentity, suppliers pass prompt=none to NHS login, ConsentNotGiven must be handled, site links require coordination, conventional downloads do not work in web integrations, and browser print is not planned
applied: tracks 375, 379, 380, 382, and 384 must own SSO redaction, consent-denied recovery, site-link coordination, file handling, and unsupported-browser behavior explicitly
rejected: launching bridge or file behavior before capability and artifact guardrails are frozen

borrowed: NHS login SSO uses asserted_login_identity and prompt parameters, and asserted tokens are time-limited one-time tokens
applied: track 379 launch packet requires single-redemption SSOEntryGrant, AuthBridgeTransaction, IdentityAssertionBinding, and ReturnIntent fences
rejected: treating raw assertedLoginIdentity as safe to persist or log

borrowed: trace evidence can include actions, screenshots, DOM snapshots, console, network, and metadata
applied: the 373 board proof uses deterministic browser interaction and is validated as a local gate artifact; heavier trace retention is reserved for failed or retry evidence
rejected: always-on trace capture for every passing static gate-board run

borrowed: browser contexts provide isolated clean-slate runs
applied: the 373 Playwright proof runs the board in a fresh context and checks interactive state deterministically
rejected: sharing browser state as release-gate evidence

borrowed: automated accessibility checks are useful but incomplete
applied: manual assistive testing remains a Phase 7 constraint even though the board verifies keyboard, semantic, and reduced-motion affordances
rejected: claiming NHS App accessibility approval from automated local checks alone

borrowed: ARIA snapshots can represent accessibility tree structure for regression review
applied: future Phase 7 testing rows reserve ARIA and semantic proof for embedded routes
rejected: using visual screenshots as the sole proof of embedded accessibility
